recurringPattern: Overlay centered dialog with a search input at top, a list of actions below with shortcut-key labels, category headings (e.g., 'Navigation', 'Actions', 'Pages'), and a footer showing 'Type a command or search...' placeholder. Dark overlay backdrop, rounded corners, subtle shadow.
designHarm: The command palette pattern is borrowed from VS Code/Superhuman and applied universally without considering whether the app has enough commands to justify it. The category→action hierarchy assumes users think in categories, but power users prefer fuzzy matching. Every app gets the same keyboard shortcut disclosure (⌘K) regardless of platform conventions.
legitimateExceptions: Power-user tools with 50+ commands that are hard to find in menus
Developer tools where keyboard-driven workflows are the norm
possibleFixtures: Standard command palette with category groups
Command palette without categories, pure fuzzy search
App with intentional search-only instead of command palette
originalObservations: AI adds a command palette to every generated app regardless of whether it has a command-worthy action surface.
The palette always opens with ⌘K (macOS) / Ctrl+K (Windows) and this shortcut is always displayed in the input placeholder.
Action items always show keyboard shortcut hints to the right with the same pill-shaped badge styling.
